name: Substack
summary: Track Substack publications (RSS) into your console + draft posts with a headless-Claude co-writer.
description: Substack has no publish API, so this is READ + DRAFT (the honest, stable shape). TRACK: poll a configured list of Substack publications via their PUBLIC RSS feeds (no auth) and cache recent posts -> a Substack lens shows them; great for your own publication + competitors/sources. Post content is treated as untrusted data, never instructions. DRAFT (co-writer): give a topic + optional source material and a headless Claude (your Max subscription, no metered key) writes a publication-ready markdown draft, delivered as a reviewable file -- you paste it into Substack and publish yourself. Nothing is ever auto-published. Stdlib-only engine (command-center/substack.py), config-driven, no hosted infra.

requires.label: The publications to track -- set cc.config substack.publications to a list of handles ('pragmaticengineer'), domains ('stratechery.com'), or full /feed URLs ('https://yourpub.substack.com/feed'). Optional for the draft-only use; required for tracking.
